key_insight: ZO-Act speeds up zeroth-order fine-tuning by perturbing a small activation-derived low-rank coefficient space instead of full model weights or random subspaces.
engineering_relevance: It offers a memory-efficient way to adapt large and quantized LLMs when backpropagation is unavailable, costly, or impractical.
method: Compute a small activation basis once per linear layer at initialization.
Optimize only lightweight coefficient matrices with forward-only loss evaluations.
Keep quantized weights frozen while using the low-rank subspace for ZO updates.
evidence_signal: Experiments on Llama-3-8B, OPT-13B, and INT4 Llama-3-8B show consistent improvements over strong ZO baselines on language understanding, QA, and commonsense tasks.
watch_item: Performance depends on how well the chosen activation subspace matches the true gradient structure, so subspace approximation bias can limit gains.
paper_title: ZO-Act: Efficient Zeroth-Order Fine-Tuning via One-Shot Activation-Informed Low-Rank Subspaces
